title: ramona clevelandii
intro: Ramona clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's mountain mahogany, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to its tannins and other compounds. The health benefits associated with Ramona clevelandii include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Ramona clevelandii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional folk remedies, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
